tests_written: 7 column-level + 1 combination uniqueness + 1 not-null on retention_rate

root_cause.headline: First-gen STEM students enrolled in MATH 220 with a Wednesday-evening discussion section show 41% lower term GPA
root_cause.detail: The section-time conflict with campus part-time-work shifts is the lever. 34 of the 83 first-gen engineering fallout students enrolled in MATH 220 sections meeting Wednesday evenings (17:00–21:00). Campus employment data shows 91% of first-gen students in those sections hold on-campus jobs with Wednesday evening shifts. GPA in the MATH 220 sections meeting Monday/Wednesday morning is statistically indistinguishable between first-gen and continuing-gen. The fix is a section reschedule, not a curriculum redesign.
root_cause.affected_cohort: First-gen STEM students in MATH 220 Wednesday-evening sections, Fall 2025

without_wizard.label: Without dbt-wizard
without_wizard.summary: Question enters the analytics backlog. Board of Trustees convenes without the answer.
narrative: Provost files the request. It enters the analytics backlog at priority B.
Institutional Research scopes the work Monday — three days after the board meeting.
Engineer writes SQL from scratch, joining four silver marts by hand.
Tests, YAML, and contract get bolted on at PR time. Sometimes.
Provost cannot isolate root cause before the board convenes.
Board receives a note: retention analysis pending.

with_wizard.label: With dbt-wizard
with_wizard.summary: Four sub-agents surface the upstreams, author the SQL, write the tests, and materialize the asset in 90 seconds.
narrative: Explorer runs status and search. Returns the four silver upstream candidates.
Summary runs describe and lineage. Confirms join path and names the new grain.
Worker runs dbt_show on the Fall 2025 first-year cohort. Validates the proposed grain.
Worker authors fct_retention_by_school_persona_term.sql with CTEs and final SELECT.
Verification writes the YAML — description, schema contract, 9 tests, ownership.
Worker materializes the table to Iceberg. Verification confirms downstream lineage.
Provost presents root-cause finding to Board of Trustees 22 hours ahead of schedule.

hero.label: Root cause identified 22 hours before the Board of Trustees convenes
hero.note: From Provost question to materialized Iceberg gold table — versioned, tested, documented, lineage-tracked. The engineering school now knows the MATH 220 Wednesday-evening section-time conflict is the gap. The fix is a reschedule, not a curriculum redesign. $6M tuition revenue exposure per cohort is now addressable with a calendar change.
